research_findings: Context-aware rule loading with step-level caching is effective for agent workflows
Progressive disclosure patterns minimize context overload while maintaining relevance
Hybrid static analysis with runtime detection provides balanced approach
Hierarchical cache structures enable granular invalidation and reuse
task_description: Create a system for finding rule scope to create a cache which can be loaded before steps so they don't repeat errors that rules have been created to avoid
requirements_gathered: Rule scope detection: Model reasoning based on current task context
Cache structure: Agent name & workflow step combination
Cache loading: Start of each step
Cache invalidation: Clear after workflow completion
Integration: Will use rule caching system
outcome: Workflow closed before decision phase. User requested closure after reviewing implementation options for rule scope caching system.
notes: Architect recommended Option 1 (Context-Aware Rule Cache with Step-Level Loading) based on research findings and user requirements. User requested closure before selecting an option.
